data.title: Traton falls after it flags lower margins for key units, weak Europe
data.description:
** Shares in Traton fall 5% after the German truckmaker flagged lower margins at its key units Scania and MAN, and prolonged weakness on the European market at a fourth-quarter pre-close call late on Thursday

** Following the call, Jefferies analysts lowered their annual earnings before interest and taxes (EBIT) expectations to 0%/-3% and their PT on Traton by 14% to 32 euros

** In particular, the analysts say that Scania will be impacted by costs coming from its China plant ramp-up, and MAN by weaker Europe and a software issue

** On the brighter side, they mention Traton's optimistic outlook for North and South America, which will drive profits up at its U.S. brand International

** Peers Daimler Truck and Volvo are also trading lower, both at around -1.5%

data.title: China Cinda Unit Files Winding Up Petition Against Sunac China
data.description:
China Cinda Asset Management unit China Cinda (HK) Asset Management filed a winding up petition against Sunac China , according to a Friday disclosure with the Hong Kong Exchange.

The hearing is set for March 19, according to the filing.

Earlier, Sunac China reportedly told some creditors it had difficulty paying part of its restructured bonds due in September, according to a Reuters report.

The indebted developer restructured offshore debt worth $9 billion in November, following a debt crunch in 2021, the report said.

data.title: Albanian banks' NPL ratio slightly down in Nov
data.description:
TIRANA (Albania), January 10 (SeeNews) - The non-performing loan (NPL) ratio of Albania's banking system stood at 4.48% at the end of November, a slight decrease from the 4.60% registered at the end of October, the central bank said on Friday.

The NPL ratio recorded a decrease on an annual comparison basis, from 5.07% in November of 2023, the central bank said in a data release.

The data refers to the loan portfolios of eleven banks operating in Albania.

data.title: India names Diageo, Sequoia Capital in case alleging suspicious payments
data.description:
Key points:
  • Diageo, Sequoia Capital face influence-peddling investigation
  • Diageo made payment to lift ban on duty-free items -agency
  • Sequoia made payments for investment approval -agency

India's federal investigating agency has accused liquor giant Diageo and Sequoia Capital of making suspicious payments to a politician's firm to seek favourable government decisions, it said in a document Reuters reviewed on Friday.

In the document, the Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) says Diageo Scotland made a suspicious fund transfer to Indian politician Karti Chidambaram's firm after a 2005 ban on sale of its duty-free products hit sales of its Johnnie Walker whisky.

In the case document on its website, the agency said an investigation showed a payment of $15,000 to the firm was intended to influence public servants to lift the ban on Diageo, rather than for consultancy work as it had been described.

"In order to lift the ban, Diageo Scotland approached Karti P Chidambaram," the agency said in the document, part of its formal case registered against Diageo and Sequoia, after an investigation launched in 2018 into investment approvals.

It did not say when the alleged payment by Diageo was made.

The agency said Diageo suffered a huge loss from the 2005 embargo on its products by the India Tourism Development Corp, a firm majority-owned by the government that had a monopoly on sales of imported duty-free liquor.

Telephone calls to seek comment from Karti Chidambaram, the son of former finance minister P Chidambaram, went unanswered. A member of India's main opposition Congress party, he is a lower house MP.

A spokesperson for Diageo's India unit, United Spirits, in which the British giant owns a stake of about 56%, declined to comment. Diageo's British unit did not immediately respond to a request for a comment on the development.

The case presents a new challenge for Diageo after anti-graft police launched an investigation last year into billing and discount practices in the capital, New Delhi.

The company has said it is cooperating with that agency.

Separately, the CBI case document accused the Mauritius unit of Sequoia Capital of suspicious transactions with the firm of Karti Chidambaram, who was in a position to influence public servants to secure approval for an Indian investment in 2008.

Sequoia's proposal, the CBI said, was approved in November by Karti's father, P Chidambaram, who was finance minister at the time. In response to a text message from Reuters, P Chidambaram, also a Congress politician, said he had no comment.

The CBI case document does not name the former finance minister as one of those accused in the case.

Sequoia did not immediately respond to a Reuters request for comment on the accusations.

A detailed investigation will look for any violations of India's criminal law, including its anti-graft law that sets penalties for bribes to public servants, the CBI added in the document.

Punishments can be jail terms of up to seven years, and unspecified fines.

data.title: Malaysian Shares Rebound on Week's Close; Green Packet's Shares Soar Over 33%
data.description:
Malaysian shares rebounded to close in the green zone on Friday following the release of upbeat economic data.

The benchmark FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI, the main gauge of Malaysian stocks, gained 1.60 points to end 0.10% higher at 1,602.41. The day range was between 1,599.71 and 1,605.74.

In economic news, Malaysia's unemployment rate stood at 3.2% in November, unchanged from a month earlier, according to data released by the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DoSM) on Friday.

The sales in the manufacturing sector in Malaysia grew 4.5% in November 2024 to 161.9 billion ringgit, faster than a 3% rise in the previous month, the DoSM said.

Malaysia's industrial production index rose 3.6% in November 2024, faster than a 2% rise in the previous month, the department said.

In corporate news, shares of Green Packet surged over 33% after its unit Packet Interactive secured a digital lending license from Malaysia's Ministry of Housing and Local Government. The license is valid until Aug. 14, 2026, and enables the company to expand its digital financial services

Shares of Texchem Resources' jumped over 3% on close after its dormant step-down unit, Texchem Food Materials (Vietnam), secured its members' approval for its dissolution.

data.title: Taiwan Mobile Says December Revenue +0.44% Y/Y At T$17.16 Bln
data.description:
Taiwan Mobile Co Ltd :

  • SAYS DECEMBER REVENUE +0.44% Y/Y AT T$17.16 BILLION ($520.49 million)

($1 = 32.9690 Taiwan dollars)

data.title: These Stocks Are Moving the Most Today: Allstate, Nvidia, IonQ, Edison International, Delta, and More — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Joe Woelfel

Stock futures declined Friday ahead of the U.S. employment report for December. Economists expect the U.S. to have added 153,000 jobs last month.

U.S. stock markets were closed Thursday in honor of former President Jimmy Carter, who died Dec. 29, at age 100.

These stocks were poised to make moves Friday:

Nvidia was down 1.1% in premarket trading following a Bloomberg report that said the Biden administration plans to impose a final round of chip export restrictions before leaving office. The plan would see U.S. allies given free rein to import chips but block imports by unfriendly countries such as China and Russia, according to Bloomberg, which cited people familiar with the matter. Fellow chip makers also traded lower. Advanced Micro Devices fell 2% and Broadcom slipped 0.7%.

Shares of Micron Technology fell 0.7% in premarket trading. Coming into Friday, the memory and storage company has risen 18% this year, making it the top stock in the S&P 500.

Edison International has been the index's worst stock in 2025, down 13%. Making up most of that decline was the power producer's drop of 10% on Wednesday after the company's Southern California Edison subsidiary began shutting off power to some customers in California in an effort to help curb the multiple fires in Los Angeles. Edison said it doesn't serve the Palisades area, where one of the largest fires occurred. But it does have some infrastructure around the sites of the Hurst and Eaton fires and has been reviewing the incidents. The stock was down 1% in premarket trading.

Allstate, Travelers, and Chubb likely will be the publicly traded insurers most exposed to losses from the L.A. fires, according to analysts at J.P. Morgan, who estimated economic losses from the fires at about $50 billion, with insured losses estimated at $20 billion or more. Allstate was down 5.1% in premarket trading, Travelers fell 4%, and Chubb declined 3.6%.

Rigetti Computing was rising 6% in premarket trading, D-Wave Quantum gained 3.1%, and IonQ jumped 11% as the quantum-computing stocks clawed back sharp losses suffered earlier in the week. The shares tumbled after Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said "very useful quantum computers" were decades away. Alan Baratz, CEO of D-Wave Quantum, however told CNBC on Wednesday that his company's services were being used by clients such as Mastercard "not 30 years from now, today."

Shares of Jefferies Financial fell 3.9%. The investment bank on Wednesday reported fourth-quarter earnings that rose 212% from a year earlier to $205.7 million and revenue that jumped 63%. The company said investment-banking revenue rose 73% in the quarter to $986.8 million.

Penguin Solutions, the provider of solutions for the computing, memory and LED markets formerly known as Smart Global Holdings, was rising 16% after posting fiscal first-quarter adjusted earnings that topped analysts' estimates and affirming its fiscal-year outlook.

Costco Wholesale reported sales in December rose 10% to $27.52 billion, same-store sales were up 7.4%, and e-commerce sales jumped 34%. Shares of the warehouse retailer gained 1.7% in premarket trading.

IGM Biosciences was falling 66% following an announcement that it would be cutting 73% of its workforce after halting further development of imvotamab and IGM-2644, two IgM-based bispecific antibody T cell engagers for autoimmune diseases.

Earnings reports are expected Friday from Delta Air Lines, Constellation Brands, Walgreens Boots Alliance, WD-40, and Tilray Brands.

Delta fell 0.9% in premarket trading ahead of the release of the carrier's fourth-quarter earnings. Analysts on Wall Street expect Delta to report earnings in the period of $1.76 a share on revenue of $14.2 billion.

Write to Joe Woelfel at joseph.woelfel@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Singapore Shares Fall, Tracking U.S. Futures — Market Talk
data.description: Singapore's FTSE Straits Times Index fell 1.6% to 3801.56, mirroring declines in U.S. futures. The widespread losses in U.S. equity futures may have dampened investor sentiment and reduced risk appetite for Asia as markets await the release of U.S. jobs data later today, IG market strategist Yeap Jun Rong writes in a note. Among the biggest decliners on the benchmark index, Yangzijiang Shipbuilding and Sembcorp Industries both dropped 3.5%, while Keppel fell 2.6%. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

data.title: Italian Monthly Retail Sales Down 0.4% in November 2024
data.description:
Italy's retail sales fell 0.4% month over month in November 2024 after a 0.5% decline in the previous month, the Italian National Institute of Statistics said Friday.

Analysts estimated a 0.2% rise for the month.

On a yearly basis, retail sales were up 1.1%, following a 2.6% increase earlier.

data.title: OSG's Full-Year Profit Slides 6%
data.description:
OSG's attributable net income for the year ended Nov. 30, 2024, declined 6.1% to 13.4 billion yen from 14.3 billion yen in the previous fiscal year.

Basic earnings per share was 148.94 yen, down from 149.29 yen in the prior year.

The industrial machinery maker's net sales rose 5.3% year over year to 155.5 billion yen from 147.7 billion yen.

The company declared a final dividend of 32 yen per share, a Thursday bourse filing stated.

The company expects an attributable net income of 14.5 billion yen, EPS of 170.75 yen, and net sales of 160 billion yen for the fiscal year ending Nov. 30, 2025.

Shares of the company closed nearly 6% lower on Friday.

data.title: Vedanta Resources gets $300 million loan commitment for debt refinancing
data.description:
Indian miner Vedanta's parent on Friday said it has secured commitments from Barclays, First Abu Dhabi Bank, and Mashreq totaling $300 million in loan tenors of 3 years and 3 months.

The proceeds will be used for refinancing some outstanding 2024 and 2026 bonds and for meeting other debt servicing obligations, Vedanta Resources said in an exchange filing.

The company is looking to upsize the deal and is in advance discussions with other banks for additional loans of $200 million, it said.

Rating agencies S&P Global and Moody's upgraded Vedanta Resources last year.

The London-based company has been shoring up its finances.

In September 2024, Vedanta Resources raised $900 million in its first dollar bond issue in more than two years and later raised $800 million through another dollar bond issue in November to refinance some outstanding debt.

"The loan arrangement enhances the group's liquidity position," Vedanta Resources said.

Its net debt stood at $11.36 billion as of September 2024, down from $12.35 billion in March, according to a company presentation.

data.title: Streetwise: Bonds Are Worth Revisiting, Now They Yield More Than Cash — WSJ
data.description:
By James Mackintosh

Benchmark 10-year Treasurys yield more than cash, and have done for three weeks. Those who've been parking their money in the safety of cash-probably via a deposit account or money-market fund-should be looking at whether the extra yield now on offer in bonds makes it worth the risk of a further selloff.

The bond risks are serious: Yields might rise further (pushing down prices) if President-elect Donald Trump's tax cuts, deregulation and tariffs boost the economy or prove inflationary, as many economists fear. But as I discuss in my latest Streetwise column, longer-dated Treasury yields have gone up a long way in a short period of time, and now look quite attractive.

Treasury bonds have no risk of default, but they only pay back in full if held to maturity-so they are not like cash. Still, if you think future interest rates will be lower and inflation will come back under control, or that there might be a downturn that forces the Federal Reserve to cut, they offer a potential for gain that cash doesn't offer, as well as a decent yield.

Here's the chart, using one-month T-Bills as a proxy for the interest rate on cash:

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Buy TCS; target of Rs 5000: Motilal Oswal
data.description:
Motilal Oswal's research report on TCS

TCS reported revenue of USD7.5b in 3QFY25, down 1.7% QoQ in USD terms vs. our estimated decline of 0.3%. Growth was driven by India (up 8.2% QoQ/66.3% YoY) and MEA (up 7.7% QoQ), while North America was down 1.5% QoQ. EBIT margins came in line with our estimate at 24.5% (up 40bp QoQ). PAT was up 4.1% QoQ/12.1% YoY at INR124b (below our est. of INR127b). For 9MFY25, revenue/EBIT/PAT increased by 6.2%/7.3%/8.5% compared to 9MFY24. In 4QFY25, we expect revenue/EBIT/PAT to grow by 7.2%/7.2%/9.5% YoY. TCS reported a deal TCV of USD10.2b, up 18.6% QoQ and 25.9% YoY. The book-to-bill ratio was 1.4x.

Outlook

We reiterate our BUY rating on TCS with a TP of INR5,000, implying a 24% potential upside.

For all recommendations report, click here

data.title: Mazda Introduce the All-NEW "Mazda6" Battery EV in Europe
data.description:
HIROSHIMA, Japan, Jan 10, 2025 - (JCN Newswire) - Mazda Motor Europe, responsible for the European operations of Mazda Motor Corporation (Mazda), today announced the introduction of "Mazda6e," the all-new Battery EV (BEV) in Europe. Premiered at the Brussels Motor Show 2025 in Belgium on January 10, the Mazda6e will go on sale summer of this year.

Mazda6e is the European version of the Mazda EZ-6, the midsize sedan BEV unveiled at the Beijing Auto Show in April 2024. The all-new BEV is designed to attain the driving performance and functionality to meet European customers' needs.

The Mazda6e was developed through the collaboration between Mazda and Changan Automobile Co., Ltd, Mazda's 20-year partner in China. The Mazda6e BEV boasts Mazda's signature design, craftsmanship and driving performance, while coming with Changan Automobile's electrification and smart-cabin technologies.

Based on the design theme, "Authentic Modern," the Mazda6e's simple and flowing coupe form expresses the automobile's inherent charm coupled with a novelty unique to electric vehicles. The meticulously-tuned braking and handling performance, which respond linearly to driver input, produce Jinba Ittai-a sense of "car and driver as one." The Mazda6e also features smart functions, including an intelligent drive system that provides support for the driver as well as accident mitigation technology and smart cabin with voice, touch, and gesture controls, all of which is aimed to raise the bar for safety and convenience.

Mazda will continue to pursue the 'Joy of Driving' under our core Human Centric value, and aim to deliver the 'Joy of Living' by creating moving experiences in our customers' daily lives.

About Mazda's Brand Essence "Celebrate Driving"

Mazda's Brand Essence is "Celebrate Driving". "Celebrate Driving" delivered by Mazda is not just about driving performance. Choosing a Mazda prizes the owner with confidence and pride. Driving a Mazda leading up to urge to take on new challenges. Not just our products but every encounter with Mazda evokes the emotion of motion and makes customers' hearts beat with excitement. All of these are contained in our brand essence of "Celebrate Driving". For more information, visit https://www.mazda.com/.

Source: mazda

Copyright 2025 JCN Newswire . All rights reserved.

data.title: Tianjin Bohai Chemical Subsidiary Gets Certified as High-Tech Enterprise in China
data.description:
Tianjin Bohai Chemical subsidiary Magnetic Card Technology was certified as a high-tech enterprise by issuing authorities in Tianjin, China.

The certification was issued Oct. 31, 2024, and is valid for three years, according to a Friday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

As a high-tech enterprise, Magnetic Card Technology will benefit from a reduced corporate income tax rate of 15%.

Shares of the chemical and pesticide producer closed 4% lower Friday.

data.title: Ikonisys Plans Capital Increase via Debt Offset, Share Issue
data.description:
Ikonisys intends to increase capital by 300,000 euros by offsetting debt and issuing new shares to improve its net financial position and expand its shareholder base.

The offset transaction relates to the Paris-listed cancer diagnostics-focused company's debt owed to Dutch investment company, Cambria KOP and an issue of 196,080 shares at 1.53 euros apiece, according to a Friday release.

data.title: Zai Lab Licenses MediLink Therapeutics' Antibody-Drug Conjugate Platform
data.description:
Zai Lab said late Thursday it has struck a licensing agreement to use MediLink Therapeutics' Tmalin antibody-drug conjugate platform for the development of its ZL-6201 investigational drug.

Financial terms were not disclosed.

Zai Lab said the partnership will expand its oncology pipeline with another drug targeting multiple solid tumors.

data.title: Gold heads for best week in seven; US jobs data on radar
data.description:
Key points:
  • U.S. payrolls report due at 1330 GMT
  • Cooling U.S. jobs data may boost gold to $2700, analyst says
  • Silver, platinum, palladium poised for weekly gains

Gold prices scaled a four-week peak on Friday and were set for their best week in seven, driven by safe-haven demand amid uncertainties over President-elect Donald Trump's policies, while markets awaited jobs data for clues on the trajectory of U.S. interest rates.

Spot gold climbed 0.4% to $2,679.91 per ounce, as of 0915 GMT after hitting its highest level since Dec. 13 earlier in the session. Prices have risen over 1% so far this week.

U.S. gold futures rose 0.6% to $2,705.90.

The most bullish outcome for gold is the pre-election narrative of ballooning fiscal deficits and unsustainable debt weighing on the U.S. dollar in the longer term and causing doubts about its role as the world’s reserve currency, said Carsten Menke, analyst at Julius Baer.

Trump will take office on Jan. 20 and has vowed to implement tariffs, which could potentially ignite trade wars and inflation.

Bullion is considered a hedge against inflation but higher interest rates blunt its appeal as it yields no interest.

The short-term focus is on the U.S. payrolls report due at 1330 GMT. According to a Reuters survey, non-farm payrolls are expected to have increased by 160,000 in December, following a jump of 227,000 in November.

"A stronger-than-expected NFP report that further dilutes expectations for Fed rate cuts in 2025 could prompt some immediate paring of gold’s recent advance. If the U.S. jobs market shows signs of cooling, perhaps allowing the Fed to ease off on its hawkish stance, that could send gold closer to $2,700," said Exinity Group chief market analyst Han Tan. (FEDWATCH)

Meanwhile, gold discounts in India widened as consumers refrained from buying as local prices rose, whereas the upcoming Lunar New Year stimulated buying in other major Asian markets.

Spot silver firmed 0.5% to $30.28 per ounce, platinum gained 0.6% to $964.64 and palladium added 1.7% to $942.28. All three metals were headed for weekly gains.

data.title: Market Chatter: China's Unisplendour Preparing for Hong Kong Listing
data.description:
Beijing-based Unisplendour is gunning for a secondary listing in Hong Kong within 2025, Bloomberg News reported Thursday, citing people familiar with the matter.

The Shenzen-listed cloud computing and information technology infrastructure company could raise up to $1 billion from the offering, the report said.

Unisplendour has called on banks to help with the listing, the report said, citing the sources.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Innate Pharma to Focus on NK Cell Therapies, ADC Development to Accelerate Growth
data.description:
Innate Pharma said Friday it is prioritizing its antibody-based NK Cell Engager platform to target hematologic cancers, solid tumors, and autoimmune diseases.

The company said it is currently testing its NK Cell Engager, IPH6501, in a phase 1/2 trial for non-Hodgkin's lymphoma.

Additionally, Innate said it is advancing its Antibody-Drug Conjugate programs, with lead candidate IPH4502 in a phase 1 trial for advanced solid tumors.

Innate is also seeking a partner for lacutamab in T-cell lymphomas and collaborating with AstraZeneca on monalizumab, which is in a phase 3 trial for non-small cell lung cancer.

data.title: Singapore Shares Retreat Further as Banking Stocks Register Losses; BH Global Plunges 39%
data.description:
Singapore's stock market remained in the red on Friday, as banks incurred losses during the day while the US held a day of mourning for the passing of the country's 39th president, Jimmy Carter.

The Straits Times Index (STI), a key benchmark for the Singapore Exchange, ranged between 3,785.680 and 3,843.35 throughout the day. It ended the session at 3,801.560, down 61.04 points or 1.58% compared to Thursday's close.

In company news, shares of BH Global plunged over 39% after it commenced the dissolution process of its indirect subsidiary in Florida, US, Omnisense Systems USA.

Singapore Land was down over 1% after it completed the acquisition of a 50% stake in 388 George Street in Sydney, Australia, for AU$460 million alongside UOL Group .

Meanwhile, S&P Global Ratings assigned an investment grade BBB- long-term issuer credit rating with a stable outlook to Stoneweg European Real Estate Investment Trust , formerly Cromwell European Real Estate Investment Trust.

data.title: Holcim's Appointment of Jenisch as CEO of Spinoff Unsurprising — Market Talk
data.description: Holcim's appointment of Jan Jenisch as chief executive and chairman of the board of the planned North American business spinoff isn't surprising, Vontobel's Mark Diethelm says. The Swiss building materials company proposed appointing Kim Fausing as Jenisch's successor as Holcim's chairman of the board. "The proposed appointment of the seasoned veteran Kim Fausing as his successor is an ideal solution for the future development of the Holcim Group," the analyst writes in a research note. Share trade 0.7% lower at 88.42 Swiss francs. (nina.kienle@wsj.com)

data.title: Chinese Maritime's UK Unit Buys $16 Million Stake in China Energy Shipping
data.description:
Chinese Maritime Transport said its UK unit, Chinese Maritime Transport (UK) acquired 16 million shares in China Energy Shipping for $16 million.

The share acquisition is for investment purposes, according to a Friday Taiwan Exchange filing.

data.title: Amylyx Pharmaceuticals Launches Stock Offering
data.description:
Amylyx Pharmaceuticals said late Thursday it has commenced an underwritten public offering of common shares.

Amylyx plans to give the underwriter a 30-day option to buy up to an additional 15% of the shares offered.

The company said the proceeds will be used to support commercialization, pipeline programs, working capital and general corporate needs.

data.title: Intuit Price Target Maintained With a $765.00/Share by Piper Sandler
Cellnex Hires JPMorgan to Explore Sale of Its Stake in Swiss Subsidiary, Expansion Says, Citing Sources
data.description:
• Cellnex hired U.S. bank JPMorgan as an advisor to explore the potential sale of its 72% stake in its Swiss subsidiary, Spanish newspaper Expansion reports, citing unnamed sources.
  • The Spanish telecommunications-infrastructure company could raise more than 1.1 billion euros ($1.13 billion)in proceeds from the sale, Expansion says.
  • Cellnex declined to comment, the report says.

Full story: https://tinyurl.com/58uuce7j

Write to Najat Kantouar at najat.kantouar@wsj.com

data.title: Coor Service, PostNord Extend Facilities Management Deal
data.description:
Coor Service Management renewed its integrated facilities management contract with the Swedish state-owned postal services group PostNord.

The two-year renewal, effective July 1, is worth 155 million kronor per year, with an option to extend for another two years, according to a Friday release.

The Swedish facility management services company will provide cleaning, outdoor maintenance, internal waste management, coffee and water services, and general janitorial services for PostNord's Nordic properties covering 2,000,000 square meters.

data.title: Global Petroleum Completes Retail Offer
data.description:
Global Petroleum raised 51,050 pounds sterling through a retail offer of shares with existing shareholders.

The oil and gas company issued 22,688,888 new ordinary shares at 0.0025 pound sterling apiece, following a placing, according to a Friday release. The retail offer shares are expected to start trading on London's AIM on Jan. 16.

data.title: Enteq Shares Plunge on Cash Warning
data.description:
By Maitane Sardon

Shares in Enteq Technologies sank after the company said it may run out of cash earlier than expected amid mounting costs related to its Saber drilling technology.

Shares fell 61% to 1.20 pence in early trading, marking its lowest ever share price. Shares are currently down 89% over the past 12 months.

The specialist energy services company said a recent demonstration of its Saber tool at the Catoosa test facility in Oklahoma showed operational functionality, but identified several areas for improvement.

Enteq said it is working to begin active test drilling operations in April, but that the delay adds uncertainty to the revenue timeline as well as further costs.

"The timing of revenue previously expected to be earned is now more uncertain, and there will be costs associated with the engineering," the company said. "The current cash runway, based on current cash resources, is now expected to be shorter than previously outlined at the time of the 2024 fundraise."

Enteq continues to actively engage with potential customers, it said.

Write to Maitane Sardon at maitane.sardon@wsj.com
